chapter_question: How can curvature live at a single vertex of a cone or polyhedron?
inspection_targets: The missing sector in a cone is concentrated curvature.
Flattening the cone exposes the angular ledger.
Face normals form a spherical polygon around a vertex.
A smooth bump can collapse curvature toward a point.

source_span.focus: Uses conical and polyhedral spikes to make curvature concentration and intrinsic/extrinsic agreement visible.
source_span.label: Chapter 14
source_span.title: The Curvature of a Spike
topics: Curvature concentrated at a conical tip
Angular defect and excess for spikes
Polyhedral version of the Theorema Egregium
Discrete models for smooth curvature ideas
